steps.title: Rewrite in slope-intercept form
steps.content: Solve $ax+by=b$ for $y$:$$by=-ax+b\Rightarrow y=-\tfrac{a}{b}x+1$$So the y-intercept is $(0,1)$ and the slope is $-\tfrac{a}{b}$.

steps.title: Use 0 < a < b to bound the slope
steps.content: Since $0<a<b$, we have $0<\tfrac{a}{b}<1$, so $-1<-\tfrac{a}{b}<0$. The line has a gentle negative slope (steeper than horizontal but flatter than $y=-x$).

steps.title: Find the x-intercept
steps.content: Set $y=0$ in $ax+by=b$: $ax=b\Rightarrow x=\tfrac{b}{a}$. Since $b>a>0$, $\tfrac{b}{a}>1$, so the x-intercept is to the right of $x=1$.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
The correct graph must (i) pass through $(0,1)$, (ii) have a negative slope between $-1$ and $0$, and (iii) cross the x-axis to the right of $x=1$.
  • A) Wrong slope sign or wrong intercept — eliminated.
  • B) Slope too steep or wrong direction — eliminated.
  • C) y-intercept at $1$, gentle negative slope, x-intercept past $1$ — matches.
  • D) Wrong intercept or positive slope — eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
